item.title: Private credit firm Castlelake explores bid for easyJet
item.description: Takeover of budget airline would lead to another UK-listed company taken off the stock market

item.title: Durex sales hit by China’s condom crackdown
item.description: Wide-ranging measures to boost the country’s birth rate are denting demand for China’s leading contraceptive brand

item.title: Lula slams US move to designate Brazil drug cartels as terrorist groups
item.description: Classification reignites tensions between Donald Trump and leftwing president

item.title: Universal Music declines takeover offer from Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square
item.description: Hedge fund billionaire argued moving record company’s listing from Amsterdam to New York would unlock value

item.title: US securities regulator seeks to end corporate disclosures on climate risk
item.description: SEC looking to overturn Biden-era requirement after finding it a ‘dramatic over-reach’ of authority

item.title: Citadel Securities posts record $4.3bn in trading revenues on Iran volatility
item.description: Market maker earns $1.9bn in first-quarter net income as oil prices and Treasury rates seesawed

item.title: Asda might help break Ocado’s supermarket stalemate
item.description: Tie-up suggests the online grocery delivery group is nearing its goal after it struggled to win new partners

item.title: Dell’s shares soar 35% as US computer maker taps into AI fervour
item.description: Rally is the latest sign of Wall Street’s exuberance over companies linked to artificial intelligence

item.title: SpaceX and the ‘enshittification’ of markets
item.description: The IPO of Elon Musk’s company is a big risk for the millions of investors who have put savings into passive investing

item.title: Directors’ Deals: Aviva chief Blanc adds to stake
item.description: Insurer’s share price has fallen by 8 per cent so far this year

item.title: Stockpickers: Diaceutics, Pets at Home, Autotrader Group
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Kenyan court blocks US Ebola quarantine camp after protests
item.description: A doctors’ group in Kenya also protested about the use of their country as a ‘containment colony’

item.title: Meg O’Neill, the no-nonsense CEO steering BP through crisis
item.description: The ‘bullying’ scandal that led to chair Albert Manifold’s removal coincides with an attempted turnaround

item.title: TotalEnergies clears path for Patrick Pouyanné to stay beyond 2030
item.description: Shareholders approve higher age limits for top jobs at oil major as board begins planning for succession

item.title: French prosecutors widen probe into alleged €14bn Hermès share fraud
item.description: Three Swiss lawyers placed under formal investigation over disappearance of Nicolas Puech’s shares in the luxury group

item.title: China’s Zijin faces delays to its $4bn swoop for Allied Gold
item.description: Allied’s biggest mine is in Mali, which has arrested a number of foreign executives in recent years

item.title: Kazakhstan offers to take Iran’s uranium stockpile, watchdog says
item.description: Fate of highly enriched material is one of the thorniest issues in US-Iran talks to end war

item.title: A bumpy road to electric vehicle success
item.description: Ferrari Luce’s launch caused more heat than light but has lessons for incumbents

item.title: Rise in Neets is a ‘retirement crisis in slow motion’, pension experts warn
item.description: Delayed pension contributions could leave higher earners with a savings shortfall of £300,000

item.title: Sports media host Stephen A Smith: ‘The audience gravitated to me’
item.description: ESPN’s highest-paid star on his plans to be the next Joe Rogan, what the Democrats get wrong — and his thoughts on a possible White House run

item.title: How the global EV shift made Ferrari Europe’s most valuable carmaker
item.description: Fears of Chinese competition have savaged valuations of mass-market peers

item.title: Am I lower-value human capital?
item.description: Thanks to one CEO’s ‘inartful’ words, it’s a question we all need to consider

item.title: CVC nears deal for IFF’s $4bn food ingredients division
item.description: Private equity firms increasingly seek to carve out underperforming divisions of big corporate groups

item.title: Russian drone hits apartment building in Romania
item.description: Nato and Bucharest condemn ‘reckless’ escalation after two people were injured in Galaţi

item.title: Ocado strikes ecommerce deal with Asda
item.description: Partnership comes after British technology group suffered blow to US business

item.title: FirstFT: Russia overspends on Putin’s Ukraine war by $28bn
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Israel attacks Beirut and EU chip supply

item.title: FT Wealth: May
item.description: In this edition: The €1bn Ponzi scheme which rocked France; what football and finance have in common; the power of Christopher Anderson’s pictures; downsides to tax moves; Monaco’s many faces; London’s priciest sushi menu

item.title: Marc Rowan rolled in Hamptons lobster fight
item.description: A New York court ruling puts the Apollo chief back at ‘square one’ in a tussle with locals

item.title: Several BP shareholders found ousted chair ‘challenging’
item.description: Several large investors say it was hard to arrange meetings with Albert Manifold before his removal from oil major

item.title: Drone start-up Stark set for €2.5bn valuation in new fundraising
item.description: German company aiming to secure at least €300mn from investors
